questions.question: What type of bonds connect the two strands of a DNA molecule?

What type of mutation results in a stop codon, resulting in a truncated protein?
What type of mutation affects the entire gene, resulting in a nonfunctional gene product?

What is the enzyme that copies the information from a single strand of DNA into mRNA called?
What is the enzyme that links the new nucleotides to the growing strand of DNA called?
What type of mutation involves the insertion or deletion of multiple nucleotides in a gene?

What are the molecules that carry the amino acids to the ribosome during translation?
What type of mutation is caused by exposure to radiation or mutagenic chemicals?
What is the enzyme that links the amino acids together to form a polypeptide chain?
What is the enzyme that proofreads the newly synthesized DNA strand and fixes any errors?
What is the enzyme that links two Okazaki fragments together during DNA replication?
What is the process by which a DNA sequence is converted into a functional protein called?
What is the enzyme that creates an RNA copy of a section of a DNA strand called?
What type of mutation occurs when a single nucleotide is substituted for another?

What type of mutation results in the substitution of an incorrect amino acid in a protein?
What is the enzyme that binds to the promoter region of DNA and initiates transcription called?
What type of mutation results in the formation of a stop codon, resulting in a truncated protein?
What are the molecules that read the genetic code and bring the appropriate amino acids to the ribosome during translation?
What is the enzyme that copies the information from a single strand of DNA into a complementary strand called?
What are the molecules that recognize specific sequences of DNA and control the activity of genes called?
What is the enzyme that links the new nucleotides to the growing strand of mRNA during transcription called?
What type of mutation involves the insertion or deletion of multiple nucleotides in a gene, resulting in a shift in the reading frame?
What type of mutation is caused by errors that occur during DNA replication or repair?
What is the process by which mRNA is decoded to produce a polypeptide chain called?

What is the term used to describe a gene that has two identical alleles?
What is the term used to describe a gene that is hidden and not expressed?
What is the term used to describe a gene that is expressed over another gene?
What is the term used to describe a gene that expresses both the dominant and recessive traits?
What is the term used to describe a gene that is passed down from the mother to the offspring?

What type of inheritance pattern is seen when a trait is passed from a father to his children?

How many chromosomes are present in a human sperm cell?

What is the process of forming a new organism from one parent called?

What type of inheritance pattern is seen when a gene is located on an autosome?
What is the process of pairing up homologous chromosomes called?
What is the term for a gene that is expressed even when the dominant gene is not present?
What type of inheritance pattern is seen when one gene can express multiple traits?
What is the term for a gene that is not expressed when the dominant gene is present?
What type of inheritance pattern is seen when a trait is passed from a father to his children?
What is the term for a gene that is expressed only when the dominant gene is not present?
What type of inheritance pattern is seen when a gene is located on the X chromosome?
What is the term for a gene that expresses both the dominant and recessive traits?
What is the term used to describe two alleles that are the same?
What is the term used to describe two alleles that are different?
What is the term used to describe a gene that is expressed over another gene?
What is the process of exchanging genetic material during meiosis called?
How many pairs of chromosomes are present in a human cell?
What is the term used to describe a gene that is hidden and not expressed?
What is the process of forming new cells from one parent cell called?
